texto: Badger had been waiting with ever increasing certainty for that brown, government stamped envelope, to hit the floor with the impact of several atomic bombs; the shockwaves hitting him before the sound could penetrate his ears.
explicacao: Citação de Paul Howsley sobre temas ligados a saude mental. “Badger had been waiting with ever increasing certainty for that brown, government stamped envelop…” articula saude-mental, anxiety, benefits em registro de saude mental, com relevância para contextos como benefits e sanctions.

frase_original: Badger had been waiting with ever increasing certainty for that brown, government stamped envelope, to hit the floor with the impact of several atomic bombs; the shockwaves hitting him before the sound could penetrate his ears.

texto: He picked up the paper and read the article; it was just one of many he had read lately that portrayed the poor in an awful light. The badge had now become the symbol of the unemployed, the sick, the disabled, and the most vulnerable. Badger had noticed that the media, just like that newspaper, swirled around anybody who they deemed too lazy or too stupid to work, and it seemed, people believed what they read.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Paul Howsley: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a trabalho. O trecho “He picked up the paper and read the article; it was just one of many he had read lately that port…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que trabalho, disabled, media pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e media.
